question: When was the University of Louisiana at Lafayette founded?
reference_answer: The University of Louisiana at Lafayette was founded in 1898 as the Southwestern Louisiana Industrial Institute (SLII).

question: What is the Carnegie Classification of UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette holds the Carnegie R1 classification — Doctoral University with Very High Research Activity, placing it in the top 3% of all U.S. universities.

question: Who is the President of UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: Dr. Ramesh Kolluru serves as the President of the University of Louisiana at Lafayette.

reference_answer: UL Lafayette's official address is 104 E. University Circle, Lafayette, LA 70503.

reference_answer: The main phone number for UL Lafayette is (337) 482-1000.

reference_answer: UL Lafayette's athletic nickname is the Ragin' Cajuns.

question: What NCAA division does UL Lafayette compete in?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette competes in NCAA Division I.

question: What conference does UL Lafayette athletics compete in?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette's Ragin' Cajuns compete in the Sun Belt Conference.

question: What is the ACT score required for the Live Oak Scholarship at UL Lafayette?

reference_answer: The Cypress Scholarship awards $1,300 per semester for up to 8 semesters.

question: What is the NCLEX pass rate average for UL Lafayette nursing graduates?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette's nursing graduates have a 95% NCLEX pass rate average over 38 consecutive years.

question: Where is the Department of Chemistry located at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Department of Chemistry is located in Montgomery Hall, Room 202 at UL Lafayette.

reference_answer: Dr. Wu Xu is the Professor and Department Head of the Department of Chemistry at UL Lafayette.

reference_answer: Dr. Natalia A. Sidorovskaia, Coca-Cola/Board of Regents Endowed Professor, serves as Chairperson of the Department of Physics at UL Lafayette.

reference_answer: Dr. Sophie Plouviez serves as the Assistant Director of the School of Biological Sciences at UL Lafayette.

reference_answer: Dr. Martin Margala is the Director of the School of Computing & Informatics at UL Lafayette, holding an Endowed Chair/Eminent Scholar position and a Fulbright Distinguished Chair.

reference_answer: Dr. Lucian Dinu (Paul Barefield BORSF Endowed Professor) is the Department Head of the Department of Communication at UL Lafayette.

question: Who is the Department Head of Communicative Disorders?
reference_answer: Dr. Ryan L. Nelson (PhD, CCC-SLP, Hawthorne-BORSF Endowed Professor I) is the Department Head and Professor of the Department of Communicative Disorders at UL Lafayette.

question: Who is the Department Head of Sociology, Anthropology & Human Development?
reference_answer: Dr. David N. Yarbrough is the Department Head and Professor of the Department of Sociology, Anthropology, and Human Development & Family Science at UL Lafayette.

question: What is the total number of residence hall beds on campus at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has 3,723 beds across 9 residence halls on campus.

question: Who is the Interim Director of the School of Architecture & Design?
reference_answer: Professor William Riehm serves as the Interim Director of the School of Architecture & Design at UL Lafayette.

reference_answer: The phone number for the School of Architecture & Design at UL Lafayette is 337-482-6225.

question: When did UL Lafayette first offer classes?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette first offered classes in 1901, beginning with 100 students.

question: When did the university change its name to University of Louisiana at Lafayette?
reference_answer: The university officially changed its name to University of Louisiana at Lafayette on September 10, 1999, when President Authement signed the order during the Centennial Celebration.

question: When did UL Lafayette become the first college in Louisiana to integrate its student body?
reference_answer: In 1954, SLI became the first college in Louisiana to integrate its student body.

question: When did UL Lafayette offer its first doctoral program in computer science?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette offered its first doctoral program in computer science in 1969, one of the earliest in the nation.

question: When was UL Lafayette first accredited by SACS?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette (then SLI) was first accredited by the Southern Association of Colleges and Schools (SACS) in 1925.

question: When did all UL Lafayette athletics move to NCAA Division I?
reference_answer: In 1974, under President Dr. Ray Authement, all athletics at UL Lafayette were elevated to NCAA Division I.

question: When did UL Lafayette open the LITE Center?
reference_answer: The LITE Center (Louisiana Immersive Technologies Enterprise) opened in 2006 as the world's first completely digital 3-D data visualization facility.

question: When was the UL Lafayette Alumni Association founded?
reference_answer: The UL Lafayette Alumni Association was founded in 1904.

question: When was The Vermilion student newspaper first published?
reference_answer: The first issue of The Vermilion student newspaper was published in 1904.

question: When did the university begin awarding bachelor's degrees?
reference_answer: In 1921, when the institution became Southwestern Louisiana Institute, it was authorized to grant bachelor's degrees.

question: When were the Twentieth Century Oaks planted on campus?
reference_answer: The Twentieth Century Oaks were planted by founding President Dr. Edwin Stephens on January 1, 1901.

question: When did Dr. Ray Authement retire as president?
reference_answer: Dr. Ray Authement retired in 2008 after 34 years as president — the longest-serving president of a public university in the United States.

question: How many jobs does UL Lafayette support in Louisiana?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette supports more than 47,500 jobs in Louisiana, representing 1 out of every 60 jobs in the state.

question: How many international students attend UL Lafayette, and from how many countries?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette enrolls 450+ international students from 82 countries.

question: How many community service hours do UL Lafayette students, faculty, and staff perform annually?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette students, faculty, and staff perform more than 35,200 community service hours annually.

question: How many dollars in scholarship money does UL Lafayette award annually?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette awards $20 million in scholarship dollars annually.

question: What percentage of first-time undergraduate students received state grants or scholarships?
reference_answer: 73% of first-time undergraduate students at UL Lafayette received state grants or scholarships.

question: How many academic schools and departments does UL Lafayette have?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has 41 academic schools and departments.

question: How many colleges does UL Lafayette have?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has 9 colleges.

question: How many faculty members are in the School of Biological Sciences?
reference_answer: The School of Biological Sciences at UL Lafayette has approximately 30 faculty members.

question: How many faculty members are in the School of Computing & Informatics?
reference_answer: The School of Computing & Informatics has 30 faculty members.

question: How many undergraduate students are enrolled in the Psychology Department?
reference_answer: The Department of Psychology has approximately 600 undergraduate majors.

question: How many live oak trees are on UL Lafayette's campus?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has over 250 live oak trees on campus, including the famous Centennial Oaks.

question: What is the total estimated value of the Live Oak Scholarship?
reference_answer: The total estimated value of the Live Oak Scholarship is $45,520 (including the $3,000/semester award for 8 semesters plus freshman housing).

question: How many semesters does the Magnolia Scholarship cover?
reference_answer: The Magnolia Scholarship covers 8 semesters.

question: How many books has the Center for Louisiana Studies published?
reference_answer: The Center for Louisiana Studies has published more than 125 books on Louisiana history, folklore, music, genealogy, architecture, and culture.

question: How many residence halls does UL Lafayette have on campus?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has 9 residence halls on campus.

question: What is UL Lafayette's ranking for eco-friendliness in the U.S.?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette is ranked #25 in the United States and #195 worldwide for eco-friendliness according to the UI GreenMetric World University Rankings (2019).

question: For how many consecutive years has UL Lafayette been recognized as Tree Campus USA?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has been recognized as Tree Campus USA for 13+ consecutive years by the Arbor Day Foundation.

question: How many graduate students are in the Psychology Department?
reference_answer: The Department of Psychology has approximately 40 graduate students.

question: How many Political Science majors does UL Lafayette have?
reference_answer: The Department of Political Science has over 230 majors and graduates over 40 majors per year.

question: What college houses the Department of Criminal Justice at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Department of Criminal Justice is housed in the College of Liberal Arts at UL Lafayette.

question: Which college is home to the School of Computing & Informatics?
keywords: Ray P. Authement College of Sciences
reference_answer: The School of Computing & Informatics is part of the Ray P. Authement College of Sciences at UL Lafayette.

question: Which department does Dr. Emad Habib belong to at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: Dr. Emad Habib is a Professor in the Department of Civil & Environmental Engineering at UL Lafayette.

question: What research center does Dr. Vijay Raghavan direct?
keywords: Center for Visual and Decision Informatics
reference_answer: Dr. Vijay Raghavan is the Director of the Center for Visual and Decision Informatics (CVDI) at UL Lafayette.

question: Which college does the LHC Group · Myers School of Nursing belong to?
reference_answer: The LHC Group · Myers School of Nursing belongs to the College of Nursing & Health Sciences at UL Lafayette.

question: Which department does the SPRUCE Lab belong to?
reference_answer: The SPRUCE Lab (Social & Psychological Research on Understanding Crime Etiology) belongs to the Department of Criminal Justice at UL Lafayette.

question: Which department is Dr. Jason Maloy part of, and what endowed chair does he hold?
reference_answer: Dr. Jason S. Maloy is a Professor in the Department of Political Science at UL Lafayette, holding the Kaliste Saloom Endowed Chair.

question: Which building houses the Department of Communication at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Department of Communication is located in Burke-Hawthorne Hall at UL Lafayette, which also houses the KRVS radio station.

question: What college does the Department of Modern Languages belong to?
reference_answer: The Department of Modern Languages belongs to the College of Liberal Arts at UL Lafayette.

question: Which research institute does Dr. Henry Chu direct?
reference_answer: Dr. Henry Chu serves as Executive Director of the Informatics Research Institute (IRI) at UL Lafayette.

question: What department does Dr. Karen Smith direct, and what is her endowed professorship?
reference_answer: Dr. Karen M. Smith is the Director of the School of Biological Sciences, holding the Lafayette Parish Medical Society Endowed Professorship in Biology.

question: Who serves as Director of the Honors Program and what department are they from?
reference_answer: Dr. Emily Sandoz, a Professor in the Department of Psychology, serves as the Director of the UL Lafayette Honors Program.

question: Which college does the Graduate School report to at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Graduate School operates under the Division of Academic Affairs at UL Lafayette, overseen by the Provost.

question: What unique feature does the Fleur-de-Lis Scholarship provide for international students?
reference_answer: The Fleur-de-Lis Scholarship provides international first-time freshman students with a full non-resident fee exemption of $6,864 per semester for up to 8 semesters.

question: Which building does the Energy Institute of Louisiana operate from, and who is its Associate Director?
reference_answer: The Energy Institute of Louisiana is directed by Dr. Mark Zappi, with Dr. Rafael Hernandez serving as Associate Director.

question: Which department does Dr. Hung-Chu Lin belong to, and what major institutional role does he hold?
reference_answer: Dr. Hung-Chu Lin is a Professor in the Department of Psychology and serves as Chair of the Institutional Review Board at UL Lafayette.

question: What organization first designated UL Lafayette's online bachelor's programs as highest ranked in Louisiana?
reference_answer: U.S. News & World Report designated UL Lafayette's online bachelor's programs as the highest ranked in Louisiana (2026).

question: What are the three levels of the TOPS scholarship in Louisiana, and what does each pay?
reference_answer: There are three TOPS levels: TOPS Opportunity (pays full tuition), TOPS Performance (pays tuition plus $400/year stipend), and TOPS Honors (pays tuition plus $800/year stipend). Apply via the FAFSA.

question: What unique degree programs does UL Lafayette offer that no other Louisiana university provides?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette offers three programs unique in Louisiana: the only undergraduate industrial design degree, the only Master of Science in Informatics, and the only PhD in Francophone Studies (one of 3 worldwide).

question: How many total faculty members across all the departments of the College of Liberal Arts handle research?
reference_answer: The College of Liberal Arts encompasses 9 departments (Communication, Communicative Disorders, Criminal Justice, English, History/Geography/Philosophy, Modern Languages, Political Science, Psychology, Sociology/Anthropology/HDFS), each with multiple active research faculty. Psychology alone has over 10 research labs, and English has over 40 faculty.

question: What are all the MBA concentrations offered by UL Lafayette's B.I. Moody III College of Business Administration?
reference_answer: The MBA at UL Lafayette offers 9 concentrations: Business Analytics, Entrepreneurship, Finance, Health Care Administration (also 100% online), Human Resources, International Business, Management, Marketing, and Supply Chain Management.

question: What does a freshman need to qualify for any UL Lafayette academic scholarship?
reference_answer: To qualify for a freshman academic scholarship at UL Lafayette, students need: a 3.0+ cumulative non-weighted high school GPA, a 23 ACT composite/superscore (or 1130 SAT), U.S. citizenship, regular admission to the university, and all requirements must be met by May 31 of the graduating year.

question: What research labs are operated within UL Lafayette's Department of Psychology?
reference_answer: The Psychology Department runs 10+ research labs: CAP Research Lab (Cognition & Psycholinguistics), Sexual Violence Research Laboratory, BEETS Lab (Building Effective Environments through Scholarship), THRIVE Lab, CROAK Lab, DAISO Lab, Developmental Science Laboratory (DSL), TASC Lab, Louisiana Contextual Science Research Group, Data Science in Social & Community Psychology Lab, Health/Emotion/Anxiety/Trauma Lab, and Stress, Coping and Wellness Laboratory.

question: What federal and state grants can students at UL Lafayette access through the FAFSA?
reference_answer: Through the FAFSA, UL Lafayette students can access: Federal Pell Grants, Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grants (FSEOG), Iraq & Afghanistan Service Grant (IASG), Louisiana Go Grant, and TOPS (Taylor Opportunity Program for Students). All require annual FAFSA filing.

question: What concentrations are offered in the BS Computer Science program at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The BS in Computer Science at UL Lafayette offers 5 concentrations: Cloud Computing, Computer Engineering, CyberSecurity, Scientific Computing, and Video Game Design & Development.

question: What are all the undergraduate concentrations in UL Lafayette's Electrical Engineering program?
reference_answer: The BS in Electrical Engineering at UL Lafayette offers 3 concentrations: Computer Engineering, Power & Sustainable Energy, and Secure Smart Systems.

question: What annual traditions are unique to UL Lafayette campus life?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette's signature campus traditions include Lagniappe Day (canoe races, 20,000-lb crawfish boil, live music in April), Festival International de Louisiane (the largest international music and arts festival in the U.S.), Krewe of Roux (Mardi Gras masquerade ball and campus parade), and Downtown Alive! (weekly free Friday night concerts).

question: What are all the programs offered by UL Lafayette's College of Nursing & Health Sciences?
reference_answer: The College of Nursing & Health Sciences offers: Pre-Licensure BSN (4-year), Accelerated RN-to-BSN (online), UL Lafayette-Ochsner Accelerated BSN, MSN (Family NP, Nurse Educator, PMHNP concentrations), DNP, Healthcare Administration (BS), and Health Information Management (BS).

question: What recognition has the UL Lafayette nursing program received nationally?
reference_answer: The UL Lafayette nursing program is designated as an NLN Center of Excellence in Nursing Education (one of 17 nationwide), ranked among the country's best by U.S. News (2022), designated a Stellar School by the National Student Nurses' Association, and maintains a 95% NCLEX pass rate average over 38 years.

question: What are all the sub-centers within the Energy Institute of Louisiana at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Energy Institute of Louisiana contains: Center for Optimization of Petroleum Systems (COPS), Center for Energy Efficiency and Sustainable Energy (EESE), Louisiana Solar Energy Lab, Cleco Alternative Energy Center, Chemicals and Fuels Development Center, Center for Environmental Protection, and Southern Unconventional Resources Center of Excellence (SOURCE).

question: How has UL Lafayette's name changed throughout its history?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette has had four names: Southwestern Louisiana Industrial Institute (SLII, 1898–1921), Southwestern Louisiana Institute (SLI, 1921–1960), University of Southwestern Louisiana (USL, 1960–1999), and University of Louisiana at Lafayette (UL Lafayette, 1999–present).

question: What are the research focus areas of the Civil Engineering department at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Civil & Environmental Engineering department at UL Lafayette focuses on five research areas: Water Resources Engineering, Environmental Engineering, Structural Engineering, Transportation Engineering, and Civil Infrastructure Materials.

question: Who are the faculty members in the School of Computing & Informatics who work on cybersecurity, and what are their specific research areas?
reference_answer: Cybersecurity faculty in the School of Computing & Informatics include: Arun Lakhotia (Director, CCIC; Software Security, Malware Analysis), Xiali (Sharon) Hei (Medical Device Security, Biometrics, Network Forensics), Bhupendra Acharya (Internet Cybercrime, Web Security), Mehmet Tozal (Complex Systems, AI, Cybersecurity), and Shuvalaxmi Dass (Software Security, Machine Learning).

question: Which UL Lafayette colleges have programs that are the only such programs offered in Louisiana?
reference_answer: Three UL Lafayette programs are the only ones of their kind in Louisiana, spanning two colleges: the School of Architecture & Design (College of the Arts) offers Louisiana's only industrial design degree; the School of Computing & Informatics (College of Sciences) offers Louisiana's only MS in Informatics; and the Department of Modern Languages (College of Liberal Arts) offers Louisiana's only PhD in Francophone Studies.

question: What transfer scholarships are available for non-U.S. citizen transfer students, and what do they provide?
reference_answer: Non-U.S. citizen transfer students can apply for the Cayenne Scholarship, which provides a full non-resident fee exemption of $6,864 per semester for up to 8 semesters. Applicants must have 12+ non-remedial credit hours from a U.S. institution, a 2.75 GPA, no bachelor's degree, and not be a re-entry student.

question: Dr. Christoph Borst researches Virtual Reality at UL Lafayette — what lab does he direct and what building is he in?
reference_answer: Dr. Christoph Borst (Hardy Edmiston Endowed Professor II) directs the VR Lab (Virtual Reality, Haptics, Visualization) in the School of Computing & Informatics, located in Oliver Hall 342.

question: Which scholarship at UL Lafayette covers both tuition and freshman housing, and what are its full benefits?
reference_answer: The Live Oak Scholarship covers both tuition and freshman housing. It provides $3,000/semester (8 semesters) in tuition funding, $5,960/semester (2 semesters) for suite-style shared freshman housing, and optionally up to $1,200/semester in campus job earnings, for a total estimated value of $45,520.

question: What world-class research infrastructure does UL Lafayette's Biology department have access to in its Research Park?
reference_answer: The School of Biological Sciences has access to the USGS National Wetlands Research Center (WARC), the NOAA Estuarine Habitats and Coastal Fisheries Center (both in the UL Lafayette Research Park), and the Louisiana Universities Marine Consortium (LUMCON) marine lab in Cocodrie, Louisiana.

question: Which faculty member holds roles in both the Computing department and serves as University President?
reference_answer: Dr. Ramesh Kolluru holds dual roles as University President and Professor of Computer Science in the School of Computing & Informatics. His research interests include Control Systems, Health Informatics, and Social Computing, and he was the founding Executive Director of the NIMSAT Institute.

question: How does the Phi Theta Kappa Scholarship combine with a transfer student's academic credentials at UL Lafayette?
reference_answer: The Phi Theta Kappa Transfer Scholarship at UL Lafayette awards $500 per semester for 4 semesters. It requires an associate degree with a 3.0+ GPA and active Phi Theta Kappa membership. It can be combined with the Associate Degree Transfer Scholarship (which awards $950–$1,200/semester for 6 semesters) for eligible students.

question: What are the total career development resources available to UL Lafayette Engineering students through ABET accreditation and professional organizations?
reference_answer: UL Lafayette's College of Engineering is ABET-accredited across all programs, and the Civil Engineering Department has an FE (Fundamentals of Engineering) exam passage rate between 80–100% per semester. Students also participate in ASCE, AIChE, IEEE, and other professional student chapters.

question: Which UL Lafayette faculty member directs both the Center for Optimization of Petroleum Systems and serves as Graduate Coordinator in Petroleum Engineering?
reference_answer: Dr. Boyun Guo (Endowed Professor) serves as both Director of the Center for Optimization of Petroleum Systems (COPS) and Graduate Coordinator in the Department of Petroleum Engineering at UL Lafayette. He was also the 2016-2017 Distinguished Professor Award recipient.
